properties.name: Suncor Base Plant
properties.inputs: Mined oil sands ore + in-situ bitumen (Athabasca)
properties.outputs: Synthetic crude oil — Suncor Sweet Premium (SSP)
properties.process: Open-pit mining of oil sands ore → ore is crushed and mixed with hot water to separate bitumen from sand → bitumen is upgraded via thermal cracking, fluid coking, and hydrotreating into a high-quality light synthetic crude.
keyFacts: The original commercial oil sands operation in Canada — operating since 1967 (then 'Great Canadian Oil Sands').
Combines an open-pit mine, extraction plant, and upgrader on a single integrated site.
Output is Suncor Sweet Premium (SSP) — a light, sweet synthetic crude that prices near WTI.
Suncor was historically Canada's largest oil & gas company by revenue prior to TC Energy / South Bow split.
Site has gone through multiple expansion phases (Millennium, Voyageur South); some were cancelled (Voyageur North coker).

properties.name: Syncrude Mildred Lake
properties.inputs: Mined oil sands ore (Mildred Lake + Aurora mines)
properties.outputs: Synthetic crude oil — Syncrude Sweet Premium (SSP)
properties.process: Open-pit mining + hot water extraction of bitumen from oil sands ore → upgrading via fluid coking and hydroprocessing into a benchmark light synthetic crude.
keyFacts: Originally a joint venture among Imperial, Petro-Canada, Esso, and others; consolidated under CNRL after a 2017 buyout.
CNRL became operator and ~70% owner in 2017 when it acquired Marathon Oil's stake.
Largest single-site oil sands operation by mine size.
Output is marketed as Syncrude Sweet Premium (SSP) — a benchmark light SCO grade.
Mildred Lake mine has operated since 1978; nearby Aurora mine added in 2000 to extend resource life.

properties.name: Cenovus Lloydminster Upgrader
properties.inputs: Heavy oil + bitumen blend (Lloydminster heavy oil region)
properties.process: Heavy oil and bitumen blends are processed via delayed coking and hydrocracking to produce synthetic crude blend, with asphalt and diesel as byproducts. Co-located with the Husky-now-Cenovus Lloydminster refinery for further processing.
keyFacts: Heavy oil upgrader purpose-built for Lloydminster heavy oil reserves.
Originally a Husky Energy asset — became Cenovus's after the 2021 Husky-Cenovus merger.
Smaller than the Fort McMurray upgraders but strategic for processing heavy oil that's difficult to pipeline.
Lloydminster sits on the AB / SK border — historically the heart of Canadian heavy-oil production.
Co-located refinery (Lloydminster Refinery) takes the upgraded output and produces transportation fuels.

properties.name: Shell Scotford Complex
properties.inputs: Bitumen from AOSP mine (piped in) + light + heavy crude (refinery side)
properties.outputs: Synthetic crude oil + gasoline, diesel, jet fuel, petrochemical feedstocks
properties.process: Adjacent upgrader (Athabasca Oil Sands Project / AOSP) processes mined bitumen into SCO via hydroprocessing. The refinery then converts SCO and conventional crude into transportation fuels using catalytic cracking, hydrotreating, and reforming.
keyFacts: One of only two integrated upgrader-refinery complexes in Canada (Suncor Base Plant being the rough other).
AOSP upgrader processes bitumen piped in from the Albian Sands mine north of Fort McMurray.
On-site Quest CCS facility captures ~1 Mt CO2 per year — first commercial-scale oil-sands CCS project (operational since 2015).
Shell sold its mining stake (Albian Sands) to CNRL in 2017 but retained the upgrader — CNRL now supplies the bitumen.
Adjacent to Edmonton's industrial heartland; one of the largest single industrial sites in Western Canada.

properties.name: Imperial Strathcona Refinery
properties.inputs: Light + heavy crude (WCSB) + synthetic crude from Imperial's oil sands operations
properties.outputs: Gasoline, diesel, jet fuel, asphalt, lubricants
properties.process: Atmospheric and vacuum distillation, fluid catalytic cracking (FCC), hydrocracking, and reforming — a full complex refinery configuration. Produces a complete slate of transportation fuels.
keyFacts: Largest refinery in Western Canada by capacity.
Imperial Oil = ExxonMobil's Canadian arm (~70% Exxon-owned).
Processes a mix of conventional crude and synthetic crude from Imperial's own oil sands operations (Cold Lake + Kearl).
Located in east Edmonton's industrial heartland — adjacent to Suncor Edmonton refinery.
Strathcona is also Imperial's R&D campus and Western Canadian corporate office.

properties.name: Suncor Edmonton Refinery
properties.inputs: Light crude + synthetic crude (Suncor's own SCO from Base Plant + Firebag) + heavy crude
properties.process: Crude distillation + FCC + hydrotreating to produce gasoline, diesel, jet, and asphalt. Heavy oil capable; processes a significant share of Suncor's own upgraded SCO from the Base Plant.
keyFacts: Co-located with Imperial Strathcona in the Edmonton refining corridor.
Processes Suncor's own SCO (from Base Plant + Firebag in-situ) plus conventional crude.
Supplies Petro-Canada-branded gas stations (Suncor owns Petro-Canada).
Sometimes referenced as the Sherwood Park refinery (the immediate municipality).

properties.name: Co-op Refinery Complex
properties.process: Atmospheric and vacuum distillation, fluid catalytic cracking, hydrotreating, and asphalt production. Supplies Co-op-branded fuel stations across Western Canada.
keyFacts: Owned by Federated Co-operatives Limited (FCL) — the wholesale arm of the Western Canadian Co-op retail network.
Unique structure: a co-op-owned refinery — products distributed through Co-op gas stations across the Prairies and BC.
Has expanded multiple times; produces a full slate of fuels plus asphalt for Western Canadian roads.
Subject of a high-profile labour dispute in 2019–2020 with Unifor — one of the longest lockouts in Canadian history.
Strategic for prairie fuel supply — competes with major-branded refineries.

properties.name: Imperial Sarnia Refinery
properties.inputs: Light + heavy crude (Enbridge Mainline / Line 5 / Line 9 routes)
properties.outputs: Gasoline, diesel, jet fuel, lubricants, petrochemical feedstocks
properties.process: Atmospheric and vacuum distillation, fluid catalytic cracking (FCC), hydrocracking, alkylation. Petrochemical-integrated (BTX, ethylene feedstocks). Part of Sarnia's industrial corridor.
keyFacts: Sarnia is Canada's petrochemical hub — multiple refineries and chemical plants in close proximity ('Chemical Valley').
Imperial Sarnia produces fuels + petrochemical feedstocks; supplies Esso-branded stations across Eastern Canada.
Crude feed comes via the Enbridge Mainline (Line 5 / Line 9 routes through Sarnia).
Adjacent to Imperial's Sarnia chemical plant — integrated petrochemical operations.
One of the oldest continuously-operating refineries in North America (since 1898).

properties.name: Irving Saint John Refinery
properties.inputs: Imported crude (Saudi Arabia, US Gulf, North Sea — diversified post-2014)
properties.outputs: Gasoline, diesel, jet fuel — ~50% exported to US East Coast markets
properties.process: Atmospheric and vacuum distillation, FCC, alkylation, hydrocracking — a full complex refinery. Tidewater location enables imported crude inputs and exported product outputs via tanker.
keyFacts: The largest single oil refinery in Canada by capacity (~320,000 bbl/d).
Tidewater location at Saint John, NB — exports gasoline and diesel to US East Coast markets.
Owned by the Irving family (Irving Oil + J.D. Irving — one of Canada's wealthiest private business empires).
No pipeline access from WCSB — crude feed is mostly imported (historically Saudi/Mideast, now diversified globally).
Strategic role in Canadian energy security — Eastern Canada's largest refining capacity by a wide margin.

properties.name: Parkland Burnaby Refinery
properties.outputs: Gasoline, diesel, jet fuel for the BC market
properties.process: Atmospheric distillation + hydrotreating + reforming. Smaller and simpler than other Canadian refineries — meets local BC fuel demand. Light crude only (no coking or heavy oil capability).
keyFacts: The only operating refinery in British Columbia.
Owned by Parkland Corporation — Canadian fuel retailer (operates Chevron-, On the Run-, and other-branded stations).
Crude feed via Trans Mountain Pipeline (light crude only — no heavy oil capability).
Smaller and simpler than other Canadian refineries — meets BC's local fuel demand.
Facility has been operating since 1935 — one of the oldest BC industrial sites.
